title: Toolbox: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied - The error returns!
body:
I've seen this bug pop and be "resolved" but on Fedora 32 with  podman-2.0.2-1.fc32 and fuse-overlay 1.1.2 I still get this pesky `Error: failed to start container fedora-toolbox-32`

I rm'd .local/share/containers and .config/containers and killed podman but to no avail

comments.body:
Why is this filed against `containers/conmon`? This sounds like a Podman issue.

The warning here is 100% expected and normal with rootless containers. It is not indicative of a real failure. The actual failure would be contained in the Podman error message.

Ah apologies. I filed it here because the only line about an error was ` conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <error>: Failed to create container: exit status 1` so I assumed it was conmon. To be honest I'm not entirely sure what the difference between the two are. I also realize I forgot to paste more of the log:

```
conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <ndebug>: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
conmon[82305]: conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <ninfo>: attach sock path: /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6ee7eaf2d398a>
conmon[82305]: conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <ninfo>: addr{sun_family=AF_UNIX, sun_path=/run/user/1000/libpod/tmp/socket/c3e7c69b367f1e37>
conmon[82305]: conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <ninfo>: terminal_ctrl_fd: 14
conmon[82305]: conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <ninfo>: winsz read side: 16, winsz write side: 16
conmon[82306]: conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <nwarn>: Failed to chown stdin 
crun container. 
conmon[82305]: conmon c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6 <error>: Failed to create container: exit status 1
libpod-c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6ee7eaf2d398aa62184842c2f9d8a439ac1bf08ea2bf5.scope: Succeeded.
systemd[2599]: Started podman-82309.scope.
podman-82292.scope: Succeeded.
podman-82309.scope: Succeeded.
```

How would I access the podman logs? There's no service for it, toolbox -vvvv doesn't mention much about podman, especially errors, and the only lines about it in journalctl are about it succeeding.

Thanks.
Just doing a `podman start --log-level=debug fedora-toolbox-32` should be sufficient.
Transferred the issue over to podman

<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

(Alreadyy Assigned) bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Toolbox fails to enter container it just created

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `toolbox enter`

**Describe the results you received:**

[Here](https://pastebin.com/znww74PD) is the log output for `podman start --log-level=debug fedora-toolbox-32`. It seems like these two lines are relevant:

```

[conmon:d]: failed to write to /proc/self/oom_score_adj: Permission denied
 
DEBU[0000] Received: -1                                
DEBU[0000] Cleaning up container c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6ee7eaf2d398aa62184842c2f9d8a439ac1bf08ea2bf5
DEBU[0000] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...  
DEBU[0000] unmounted container "c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6ee7eaf2d398aa62184842c2f9d8a439ac1bf08ea2bf5"
Error: unable to start container "c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6ee7eaf2d398aa62184842c2f9d8a439ac1bf08ea2bf5": creating cgroup directory `/sys/fs/cgroup/net_cls/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-c3e7c69b367f1e370bb6ee7eaf2d398aa62184842c2f9d8a439ac1bf08ea2bf5.scope/container`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error

```
**Describe the results you expected:**

Container runs

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman --version
podman version 2.0.2

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.2-1.fc32.x86_64

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical, Fedora 32 with kernel 5.7.8-fsync.301.fc32.x86_64
@giuseppe, do you have a suspicion what could go wrong here?

I think you've mounted cgroup v1 controllers on a cgroup v2 host.  Could you share the output for `grep cgroup /proc/mounts ` ?

If so, it is an issue in crun, fixed with: https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/436 (looks similar to https://github.com/mullvad/mullvadvpn-app/issues/1893)

are you using mullvad?

Not Mullvad, But I have the PIA vpn client installed. Possibly same issue?
```
$ grep cgroup /proc/mounts
cgroup2 /sys/fs/cgroup cgroup2 rw,seclabel,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime,nsdelegate 0 0
none /sys/fs/cgroup/net_cls cgroup rw,seclabel,relatime,net_cls 0 0
```
yes definitely same issue, I am going to close this issue as it is not related podman and we already have a patch to address it.

Thank you so much, Uninstalling PIA and rebooting fixed it. 

I find it really interesting that a conmon warning message generated by a `toolbox` command that seemed like a podman issue was actually a crun issue caused by my VPN client.
